text:
This is a C++ issue, nothing to do with pybind11. It is caused by this statement:

```
Matrix matrix(size, std::vector<int>{size, 0});
```

constructing an inner vector of size *2*, because `std::vector<int>{size, 0}` matches the initializer\_list constructor of vector (since both `size` and `0` are ints), and so your inner vectors are size 2 (containing values `size` and `0`). (This is an annoyance and source of bugs in C++ -- the same thing can happen with the std::string constructor).

The simplest solution is to use `( )` instead of `{ }` on the inner constructor:

```
Matrix matrix(size, std::vector<int>(size, 0));
```

which will give you the expected size×size 2d vector. (And here you *can* use `{}` on the *outer* constructor, because the second argument here is not an int and so the initializer list constructor doesn't match).
